scenarios.narrative: One month of operations for a small bistro, exercising food COGS from inventory, sales tax payable, tips payable to staff, credit-card processing fees, and payroll with withholding liabilities.

entries.memo: Card sales week 2: food + 8% sales tax + tips collected
Credit card processing fee on week 2 sales (3% of 1,188,000)
Record COGS for week 2 food sales
Card sales week 3: food + 8% sales tax + tips collected
Credit card processing fee on week 3 sales (3% of 1,426,000)
Record COGS for week 3 food sales
Run payroll: gross wages, withhold taxes, net to staff
Remit collected sales tax to state

scenarios.narrative: One month for a B2B SaaS startup: annual prepaid plans booked to deferred revenue and recognized monthly, monthly subscription revenue, hosting COGS, R&D payroll, and an accrued expense.

entries.memo: Recognize 1 month of Customer A annual plan (1,200,000/12)
Recognize 1 month of Customer B annual plan (2,400,000/12)

scenarios.narrative: One month for an online retailer: inventory purchases and COGS on sale, merchant processing fees, shipping income vs shipping expense, multi-state sales tax payable, customer refunds (contra-revenue), and gift-card liability.

entries.memo: Online sales: product + shipping + sales tax (CA 9%) collected by card
Merchant processing fee on week sales (2.9% of 1,830,000 = 53,070)
Online sales: product + shipping + sales tax (TX 8.25%) collected by card
Merchant processing fee on week sales (2.9% of 2,398,000 = 69,542)
Customer refund of product (reverse sale + tax, restock not modeled)
Restock returned merchandise into inventory at cost
Redeem gift cards for product sale

scenarios.narrative: One month for a general contractor: a customer deposit, job-cost materials and subcontractor costs (AP) flowing into work-in-progress, progress billings with retainage payable held back, and an equipment purchase.

entries.memo: Receive customer deposit on new job
Job materials purchased on account into WIP
Subcontractor costs on account into WIP
Progress billing: AR + 10% retainage held by customer
Recognize job costs from WIP for billed work
Apply customer deposit against progress billing AR

scenarios.narrative: One month for a consulting firm: unbilled accrued revenue recognized then billed to AR, AR collections, owner draws (contra-equity), prepaid insurance amortization, and software subscription expense.

entries.memo: Accrue unbilled consulting revenue (work performed, not yet invoiced)
Bill clients: convert unbilled to AR
Additional consulting billed directly to AR

scenarios.narrative: One month for a nonprofit: unrestricted donations, restricted grant revenue, a deferred (conditional) grant liability later recognized, and program vs admin expenses, tracking restricted and unrestricted net assets as equity.

entries.memo: Opening unrestricted net assets (cash on hand)
Opening restricted net assets (cash restricted for program)
Conditional grant advance received (conditions unmet -> deferred)
Conditions met: recognize portion of deferred grant as revenue

scenarios.narrative: One month for a residential rental: rental income, a tenant security deposit held as a liability, a mortgage payment split into interest expense and principal reduction, straight-line building depreciation via accumulated depreciation, property tax and repairs.

entries.memo: Collect tenant security deposit (held in trust)
Mortgage payment: interest expense + principal reduction

scenarios.narrative: One month for a solo freelancer on a near-cash basis: 1099 service income, home-office and equipment purchases, supplies and mileage, an estimated quarterly tax payment treated as an owner draw, and a small owner draw.

entries.memo: Estimated quarterly tax payment (treated as owner draw)

scenarios.narrative: One month for a marketing agency: pass-through media spend billed to clients (AP to vendor, AR to client), retainer collected to deferred revenue then recognized, contractor payments, multiple unpaid client invoices (AR aging) with one partial payment, and an owner distribution.

entries.memo: Vendor media buy on account (pass-through cost)
Bill client for pass-through media + markup
Partial collection on media AR invoice
Partial collection on Client B invoice

scenarios.narrative: One month for a medical clinic: gross charges to insurance AR and patient AR, contractual adjustments as contra-revenue, supplies inventory consumed, equipment with depreciation, payroll with withholding, and a deferred patient credit (prepaid copay) liability.

entries.memo: Gross patient charges billed to insurance and patients
Contractual adjustment (insurance allowed less than billed)
Apply patient deferred credit against patient AR
Run payroll: gross wages, withhold, net cash
